terms.definition: A dimensionless composite index (0–1) representing systemic alignment across nine social domains.
Any semi-autonomous component within a larger social system whose internal dynamics contribute measurably to overall coherence.
A sequence of feedback-linked changes where perturbations in one variable amplify changes in others.
A bifurcation point in a dynamical system beyond which trajectories diverge qualitatively and become hard to reverse.
The degree of randomness or disorder in social signaling and institutional functioning.
Information input that counteracts entropy by ordering or structuring a system.
A value rescaled to the interval 0–1 using historical minima and maxima as bounds.
An average where each component contributes proportionally to an assigned weight; here all weights equal 1/9.
